## Introducing The User Engagement Top 100 Report: Mobile Gaming

**Published by**

June 1, 2017

![](https://iterable.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/060117_Introducing-The-User-Engagement-Top-100-Report-Mobile-Gaming.png)

Whenever new market research discusses mobile applications, gaming dominates the conversation. Consumers spent [$41 billion](https://www.superdataresearch.com/market-data/market-brief-year-in-review/) on mobile games in 2016, surpassing PC and consoles as the largest game segment.

Freemium games, in particular, are projected to account for [90 percent](https://www.appannie.com/insights/market-data/app-monetization-report-2016/) of market share in the app industry by 2020. Mobile gamers spend an average of $87 per year on in-app purchases and up to [$550 annually](https://intelligence.slice.com/hardly-pocket-change-mobile-gamers-spend-an-average-of-87-dollars-on-in-app-purchases/) on top grossing games, like _Game of War: Fire Age_.

How are game publishers able to keep customers so engaged? We decided to conduct a study on a popular messaging channel used to motivate gamers to play regularly: the [push notification](https://iterable.com/blog/the-5-stages-of-user-engagement-in-mobile-gaming). Utilizing push is an important component of a broader omni-channel marketing strategy, and vital to gaming as it’s typically the most relevant and direct line of communication with customers.

In [The 2017 User Engagement Top 100 Report: Mobile Gaming](https://iterable.com/mobile-gaming/), we analyzed the push messaging strategies adopted by the top 100 freemium mobile games in the U.S. to study how they communicate with their customers.

[![](https://iterableblogimport.files.wordpress.com/2017/06/dbf85-1ofkinoa5rvxqeji2aua1ha.png)](https://iterable.com/mobile-gaming/)

Here’s what you’ll find in this report:

-   **Insightful stats** about the top grossing games
-   **Case studies** with real-life examples of gaming push notifications
-   **Recommendations** on how to make your pushes stand out

With better insight into how and when mobile games use push notifications, all B2C companies can develop more effective and engaging [omni-channel marketing campaigns](https://iterable.com).

We hope you gain valuable insights from [The 2017 User Engagement Top 100 Report: Mobile Gaming](https://iterable.com/mobile-gaming/).

You can expect more reports like this to be published on a quarterly basis, covering a variety of industries and marketing channels.

Like what you see and want to learn how to put our marketing recommendations into action? [Request a demo today](https://iterable.com/demo/).
